prevalence.value: low recognized incidence; seroprevalence suggests silent exposure in some populations
prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde / published Brazilian seroprevalence studies (HEV) / WHO
prevalence.trend: likely stable; improved recognition with expanded testing in hepatitis workups

incidence.source: Ministério da Saúde hepatitis surveillance summaries

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; severe in pregnancy and immunosuppression
burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in pregnancy with genotype 1/2 outbreaks (rare in Brazil)

riskFactors: consumption of undercooked pork/wild game (zoonotic genotypes)
vulnerablePopulations: pregnant people (severe disease risk in some settings)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Anti-HEV IgM and/or HEV RNA PCR; evaluation in acute hepatitis with negative HAV/HBV/HCV

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; tested in selected hepatitis cases
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Supportive care generally sufficient; chronic HEV in immunosuppressed requires specialist management

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Testing availability varies; often diagnosed in reference centers and research-supported labs
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive management; ribavirin considered for chronic HEV in immunosuppressed under specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis due to limited routine testing and low clinician suspicion
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialist hepatology services in major centers can manage complex cases and improve detection
